task: Ralph + Team (2026-05-15): Ship a discoverable, user-friendly way to configure ALL integration auth types in /admin -> Integrations. Today only authType=oauth2 has any config UI (and only via a tiny unlabeled Key icon). secret_text, basic_auth, and custom_auth integrations have no config path at all. Add a labeled Configure button on every row, a polymorphic config form covering all 5 auth types, backend platform-level credential storage for non-OAuth auth types, bulk enable/disable, and a guided per-provider wizard for the top providers. Never bind 0.0.0.0. Never weaken security. Never touch prod. All credential writes must go through the existing envelope-encryption vault and through platformAdminMiddleware.

stories.title: Discoverable, labeled Configure action for every integration (all auth types)
acceptanceCriteria: apps/web/src/modules/integrations/integrations-admin-panel.tsx: every row in /admin -> Integrations now renders a visible button labeled 'Configure' (with key/cog icon) next to the existing 'Enable' toggle, regardless of authType
The Configure button is shown for authType in {oauth2, secret_text, basic_auth, custom_auth}; for authType='none' the Configure button is hidden (nothing to configure)
Clicking Configure on a non-OAuth integration opens a config form (modal or inline sheet) appropriate to that auth type, not the OAuth-only form
The existing tiny Key icon button is replaced or absorbed by the new labeled Configure button so there is exactly one Configure entry point per row
Row visual state still reflects: NOT CONFIGURED (gray) / ENV (blue) / CONFIGURED (green) / NEEDS-CONFIG (yellow) after the redesign
lsp_diagnostics shows 0 errors on the changed file

stories.title: Polymorphic config form covers oauth2, secret_text, basic_auth, custom_auth
acceptanceCriteria: A new component (e.g. apps/web/src/modules/integrations/integration-config-form.tsx) renders the correct fields based on the integration's authType from the registry
oauth2 form: client_id, client_secret (password input + reveal toggle), shows copy-paste redirect URI helper, optional scopes display
secret_text form: single secret field (password input + reveal toggle), provider hint from registry (e.g. 'Get your API key from platform.openai.com/api-keys')
basic_auth form: username + password fields
custom_auth form: dynamic key/value pairs driven by the integration's customAuthFields registry definition (or graceful generic fallback if not declared)
Existing secrets are NEVER pre-filled in any password field; instead the form shows a 'Replace existing credential' affordance + masked tail of stored client_id where applicable
Form submit calls the correct admin endpoint per auth type; success closes the form, refreshes the list, and the row flips to CONFIGURED state
Validation: client_id/secret/api key non-empty, no trailing whitespace, length sanity-checked client-side and server-side

stories.title: Backend platform-level credential storage for non-OAuth auth types
acceptanceCriteria: New migration adds platform_integration_credentials table: id, integration_id (unique), auth_type, credentials_encrypted (bytea), credentials_format ('pgp_sym' | 'envelope_v1'), created_by, created_at, updated_at. Mirrors the oauth_apps encryption pattern. Includes RLS policy that locks ALL access behind the API service role.

services/api/src/integrations/credential-vault.ts gains a platformCredentials API with get/list/upsert/delete that encrypts via pgp_sym_encrypt (and envelope_v1 when DOABLE_ENVELOPE_ENCRYPTION=1 with a deterministic platform KEK scope) consistent with how oauth_apps does it for global rows today

services/api/src/routes/integrations-admin.ts adds POST/DELETE/GET /integrations/admin/credentials behind platformAdminMiddleware; payload accepts authType + credentials JSON; never returns the secret in any response (returns clientId masked tail and updated_at only)
The catalog 'configured' flag now resolves true if oauth_apps OR platform_integration_credentials OR env vars provide credentials for that integration
The runtime resolution in connections-manager / vault-bridge prefers user > workspace > platform > env for non-OAuth too (matching today's oauth_apps order)

Contract test: POST /integrations/admin/credentials with a valid OpenAI secret, then GET /integrations/admin/credentials, asserts the response array contains the integration but DOES NOT contain the secret field; DELETE removes it
No 0.0.0.0 binds introduced. Migration is forward-only and idempotent (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pattern consistent with other migrations)

stories.title: Bulk select + bulk enable/disable + preset stacks
acceptanceCriteria: Each integration row in the admin panel has a checkbox; each category section has a 'select all in category' checkbox

When >=1 row is selected, a sticky action bar appears with: 'Enable selected', 'Disable selected', and a 'Configure next' button that opens the config form for the first selected unconfigured integration (after submit, advances to the next)

3 preset stacks are exposed: 'Productivity (Google + Notion + Slack)', 'Dev (GitHub + Linear + Sentry + PagerDuty)', 'Marketing (Mailchimp + HubSpot + Stripe + Calendly)'. Clicking a preset selects those rows; admin still has to click Enable selected + configure each
Bulk enable calls /integrations/admin/platform-enabled in sequence (no new endpoint), shows per-row success/failure
lsp_diagnostics 0 errors on changed files; no regression in the existing single-row enable/disable flow
Bulk operations are guarded by platformAdminMiddleware on every request (no client-trust)

stories.title: Per-provider guided wizard for top providers + audit log + security review
acceptanceCriteria: A new file apps/web/src/modules/integrations/provider-setup-guides.ts exports a Map<integrationId, SetupGuide> with entries for at least: slack, google_sheets, google_calendar, gmail, notion, github (api), linear, stripe, hubspot, openai, anthropic, mailchimp, calendly, intercom, twilio. Each guide has: consoleUrl (deeplink), steps[] (3-5 short strings), requiredScopes[] (optional), troubleshooting[] (optional)

When a user opens Configure for an integration that has a guide, the form shows: step list, a 'Open provider console' button (target=_blank), a one-click 'Copy redirect URI' button, and the credential fields. Integrations without a guide get a generic fallback ('Paste your credentials from the provider')

After successful credential save, a 'Test connection' button appears that calls a new POST /integrations/admin/test endpoint (or extends an existing one). The endpoint exists and returns { ok: true, message } or { ok: false, error }. For OAuth, this verifies tokenUrl reachability + client_id is non-empty. For secret_text/basic_auth/custom_auth, it returns ok if credentials are stored (no upstream call required in v1).

Audit log: every create/update/delete on /integrations/admin/oauth-apps and /integrations/admin/credentials writes an audit row (existing audit_log infra). Field captured: actor user_id, integration_id, action, IP, user_agent. Secret values MUST NOT appear in audit log.

Architect review (THOROUGH tier) verifies: (a) no raw secret ever serialized in API response, (b) all admin endpoints behind platformAdminMiddleware, (c) RLS on platform_integration_credentials is restrictive, (d) audit log captures every credential mutation, (e) no new 0.0.0.0 binds, (f) the polymorphic config form does not leak existing secret values into the DOM
